post.summary: Resume a paused or unfinalized run
post.description:
Resumes a previously paused or unfinalized PRD flow. Restores section state from MongoDB and continues the iteration loop from the next unapproved section.

Set ``auto_approve=true`` in the request body to resume without pausing for manual approval (same as CLI mode). The API responds immediately with 202 and the flow proceeds autonomously.

**Resume behaviour:**
- Sections that already have content skip the initial draft step and go directly into the critique→refine loop.
- If Phase 1 (Executive Summary) has ≥ PRD_EXEC_RESUME_THRESHOLD iterations, Phase 1 is skipped entirely and Phase 2 resumes from the next unapproved section.
- Any degenerate content (empty or whitespace-only) left by a previous crash is cleaned up before iteration resumes.
- Requirements approval is skipped if already completed.

The run_id must appear in the resumable list returned by GET /flow/prd/resumable.

Equivalent to selecting a run to resume in the CLI startup prompt.

404.description: Run not found in resumable runs.

500.description: Internal server error. The `error_code` field will be `INTERNAL_ERROR`. Check the `message` and `detail` fields for diagnostics.

503.description: LLM / OpenAI service unavailable. The `error_code` field will be `LLM_ERROR` (retries exhausted) or `BILLING_ERROR` (billing / quota issue). The affected flow run is automatically paused — resume it after resolving the issue.
